node.title: Dissecting the System Design of Zomato / Uber Eats

article.title: Dissecting the System Design of Zomato / Uber Eats
article.content:
I recently did a deep-dive into the architecture of large-scale food delivery platforms like Zomato and Uber Eats — analyzing how these systems scale, stay available, and deliver fast and relevant results to millions of users.

Here’s a breakdown of the key components we covered:

**High-Level Architecture & Service Decomposition**
**Clean separation of services:** Search, Order Management, Rider Assignment, Real-time Tracking
Microservices with well-defined contracts and event-driven communication

![design pic.png](https://assets.leetcode.com/users/images/617a1973-2cf8-4972-aa7d-92839090041f_1749314460.5877063.png)


**Fault Tolerance & Reliability**
Order placement and delivery tracking flows are highly available
Use of CDC pipelines, retries, distributed locks, and graceful degradation to avoid user impact

**Fuzzy & Semantic Search with Elasticsearch**
Search supports brand, cuisine, and item-based queries

Fuzzy matching for typos (e.g., “buger”) and semantic understanding (e.g., “cheesy spicy burger with coke”)

Delta encoding and custom document layout improve latency and compression

**Delivery Partner Selection via GeoHashing**
Service area is divided into geohash zones or quad-tree regions

Nearby riders are selected based on ETA, availability, and fairness using distributed state (e.g., Redis)

Distributed locks ensure no duplicate assignment

**Real-Time Data Freshness & Indexing**
Dual ingestion pipeline:

Cold path for bulk restaurant/menu ingestion

Hot path using Kafka, Debezium, and Elasticsearch partial updates for real-time changes (e.g., status, ETA)

Near-instant search reflection with refresh interval tuning

**ETA Bucketing for Precision**
Stores indexed into ETA buckets (e.g., 0–10 min, 10–20 min)

Improves ranking by factoring in delivery speed, not just popularity

Enables parallel query execution and better recall

This system is a great example of combining geo-awareness, search intelligence, and real-time systems to solve high-throughput, low-latency challenges at scale.

https://youtu.be/YDrvLsF3L20?si=eZ-UAauQEAWPFilN
